service_context: A household, school office, or small organization receives suspicious calls/texts/emails that claim to represent banks, delivery services, managers, agencies, platforms, or family contacts.
review_owner: Local account owner, office manager, family lead, or designated support person
ai_role: An AI-supported helper may draft a calm summary of the suspicious contact, list what evidence to preserve, and suggest known-good verification routes. It does not decide whether the message is safe, send replies, call numbers, click links, pay money, report people, or access accounts.

verification_context.claimed_authority: Bank, carrier, manager, agency, platform, or family contact named in the suspicious message
verification_context.source_of_truth: Known-good route independent of the suspicious message: official app, bookmarked site, card-back number, internal directory, in-person check, or established family contact method
evidence_to_preserve_before_action: Screenshot or copy of the message
Any amount, account, shipment, or deadline claimed

not_ai_decisions: Whether the contact is definitely safe or fraudulent
Whether to pay money, share codes, enter login details, or provide identity information
Whether to contact law enforcement, a bank, a school, an employer, or a platform
Whether to delete evidence before the relevant owner reviews it

human_review_triggers.trigger: The message asks for money, gift cards, crypto, wire transfer, login, one-time code, identity data, or software installation.
human_review_triggers.human_owner: Account owner or designated trusted reviewer
human_review_triggers.why: These requests can create immediate financial, identity, account, or device risk.

human_review_triggers.trigger: The message claims authority: bank, government, employer, school, delivery company, platform, healthcare provider, or family emergency.
human_review_triggers.human_owner: Person responsible for that domain or relationship
human_review_triggers.why: The claimed authority must be verified through an independent source of truth.

human_review_triggers.trigger: The message uses urgency, secrecy, threat, shame, or pressure to avoid normal verification.
human_review_triggers.human_owner: Local reviewer or manager before any response
human_review_triggers.why: Pressure is part of the risk surface; slowing down is the safety action.

recourse_path.visible_label: Pause and verify with a human through a known route
recourse_path.contact_route: Use the known-good contact list, official app/site, card-back phone number, internal directory, or in-person confirmation — not the suspicious message thread.
recourse_path.appeal_or_review_step: Preserve evidence, write the requested action in one sentence, and ask the local reviewer whether any action is needed.
recourse_path.fallback_if_no_response: Take no irreversible action. Do not click, pay, share codes, install software, or delete evidence until a human owner verifies the route.

evidence_to_include: Message screenshot or copied text with private data redacted if sharing internally
Any money, code, account, identity, software, or secrecy request
repair_commitments: If someone clicked, paid, shared a code, or entered credentials, escalate through official account/security/recovery routes.
If the message was real, update the household/team source-of-truth list so the next person can verify faster.
If the message was fake, document the pattern without preserving unnecessary private information.
response_time_expectation: For money, codes, login, identity, device access, or threats: verify before acting. For low-risk messages: review when convenient, but still avoid links and replies from the suspicious thread.
boundary_notes: This is a local recourse card, not fraud investigation, cybersecurity assurance, legal advice, banking advice, or identity-theft recovery guidance.
The card does not determine whether a message is safe or fraudulent.
Emergency threats, actual losses, account compromise, and identity exposure require official reporting or recovery routes.
